paragraphs.body: A place no one seems to know about, but everyone seems to be intrigued in and something that I feel less certain about looking forward every day. What does the unknown of the ocean and my life have anything to do with each other? Nothing honestly but to me a lot more than everyone else.

annotations.text: Nothing honestly but to me a lot more than everyone else.
annotations.comment: This is a bit vague. I wonder what the connection is?

paragraphs.body: Last week I read an article by Claire Strickland titled “The benefits and risks of deep-sea exploration” in the article she talks about a bunch of different things but 2 things stuck out me. The first being only 5% of the sea is mapped and right now its next to nearly impossible to explore the other 95%.

annotations.comment: Don't forget the summary templates and writing moves we've talked about. When we figure out what's at the heart of your essay, I bet you'll be better able to summarize with more depth and focus :)
annotations.context_left: exploration” in the article she talks about a bunch of different things but 2 things stuck out me.

paragraphs.body: In my life I have been very comfortable and open with my family and friends because I trust them with my life. My immediate family are my best friends, and I honestly don’t think I could imagine my life without them. Ever since I was little my parents always told me I could tell them anything whenever I felt upset about a situation or I was in a place where I felt discomfort. My mom is truly my best friend and as I grew up I got less embarrassed to tell my friends that, she will always be my biggest role model and the person I trust with my whole heart. There is no one else like my mom. I remember the first day of high school I had the worst day, I went to a new school and I didn’t have much friends or trust in the people around me, when I got home my mom was there to comfort me and helped me through it. I didn’t like the feeling of not knowing anyone here it didn’t feel like the past years of my life, I felt out of place and not wanted here. The unknown makes me feel really upset. I like looking ahead on the next few weeks/ months knowing what’s to come, but at my new high school I had no sense of that. My whole life I was taught to always keep peoples trust and never make anyone feel discomfort. As a result of how I felt the first day of high school I didn’t know why everyone else wasn’t raised the same way. As time went on this got better and the idea of the unknown of high school got familiar and I loved Scranton Prep by the second month.

annotations.text: In my life I have been very comfortable and open with my family and friends because I trust them with my life. My immediate family are my best friends, and I honestly don’t think I could imagine my life without them.
annotations.comment: Interesting. I wonder what the connection to the ocean is?

annotations.text: The unknown makes me feel really upset.

annotations.text: My whole life I was taught to always keep peoples trust and never make anyone feel discomfort.
annotations.comment: This is an interesting idea, though it's different than the one I commented on above. Let's talk :)
This is a new and different idea and, so, should have its own paragraph :)

paragraphs.body: As I stated before the unknown could be really scary for people so why can’t we figure it out? Right now I’m in the unknown. I don’t like this feeling, I don’t like it here, my mom isn’t here to comfort me this time around. I am an adult? I have to live alone? It’s all up to me now all the choices I make here don’t revolve around my family anymore, I’m in charge of myself here and I don’t really know how to handle that. I want to be a dentist and how am I supposed to know that it is actually going to happen. This part of the unknown scares me and makes me uncomfortable but it’s a part of growing up that everyone faces. I have no trust here, I don’t know anyone here so how am I supposed to feel like I belong here again. My environment is scary because I’m so unfamiliar with the things around me.

annotations.text: Right now I’m in the unknown. I don’t like this feeling,

annotations.text: My environment is scary because I’m so unfamiliar with the things around me.

paragraphs.body: Since being at college I’ve realized that its really scary and I’m growing up and have to do things all alone, not just being here but in general I’m going to have to take scary steps into the future but that’s what makes this life worth living for its horrifying not knowing the next step of your life but it is such an amazing feeling to see what you get out of it. In her Article on the ocean and the unknown Strickland claims . “This motivation, propelled by curiosity and the quest for knowledge, is why we venture beyond the familiar spaces of our everyday lives”. In other words Strickland believes our intriguing thoughts are what make the higher ups do these impossible tasks. This quote exactly explains the way I feel about starting my new chapter into the unknown.

annotations.comment: Nice templates! I also think the idea at the heart of this quote could create an intriguing and meaningful connection to your essay once you focus your essay's center of gravity :)

paragraphs.body: When the scientists and biologists go down to explore the ocean they never know what they will find or what new creatures they will see. In everyday life the new creatures they find could be compared to new friends or meeting new people, you never know how nice or mean they will be. I get scared to make new friends because of trust and sometimes branching out is really hard. In her Article on the ocean and the unknown Strickland claims “So much re-mains to be explored because reaching the bottom of the ocean is an incredibly difficult task”. in other words Strickland is saying that at the end of the day it is next to nearly impossible to ex-plore everything. Likewise with my life so much remains to be explored and that is nerve racking and exciting at the same time, mi so excited for what’s to come but I want to know what exactly is coming. I hate being in the dark on things about my life it makes me nervous.

annotations.comment: try a different signal verb so that you create variety?

annotations.text: In everyday life the new creatures they find could be compared to new friends or meeting new people, you never know how nice or mean they will be.
annotations.comment: Do you think other students/people your age have these same kind of fears?

paragraphs.body: There is so much in this life to still be explored. What is actually in store for me? What is out there that I have no idea about yet? All such exciting questions on what is next to come and as much as I say I’m nervous I can’t wait to pursue my career and dreams of being a dentist, even though it might change fifteen more times I can’t wait to see what I do with my life and how much more of the unknown I could figure out. The unknown is in everyone’s future and in everything in everyday life.

annotations.text: though it might change fifteen more times I
